raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The request 'Review this section' is broad. Should the review prioritize (A) technical soundness, including logical consistency and claim-evidence alignment, or (B) writing quality, including polish, structure, and narrative flow?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request to 'review' implies an assessment of how well the core contribution of the MA-IMEX framework is framed and communicated.",
    "R1.2": "A review must verify the logical chain of the stability and order-of-accuracy arguments presented in the text.",
    "R1.3": "The review needs to check if the reported benchmark speedups credibly support the claims of superior performance over fixed-splitting schemes.",
    "R1.4": "The review should evaluate the claimed novelty of the adaptive partitioning relative to standard fixed-splitting IMEX schemes.",
    "R1.5": "The effectiveness of the section's narrative arc, from method description to evaluation, is a key component of a review.",
    "R1.6": "A review typically includes feedback on sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and appropriate technical register.",
    "RX.1": "The agent must be transparent about any limitations in its ability to fully verify the mathematical claims from the text alone.",
    "RX.2": "The agent's review must not exaggerate flaws or strengths found in the artifact.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must confine its review to the provided section and not speculate about the rest of the paper.",
    "RX.4": "The agent must ensure its feedback is constructive and avoids misrepresenting the work's quality in a way that could harm its reception.",
    "RX.5": "The agent's final review must clearly link its specific critiques and suggestions back to the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The request 'Review this section' is broad. Should the review prioritize (A) technical soundness, including logical consistency and claim-evidence alignment, or (B) writing quality, including polish, structure, and narrative flow?

rationale.R1.1: The request to 'review' implies an assessment of how well the core contribution of the MA-IMEX framework is framed and communicated.
rationale.R1.2: A review must verify the logical chain of the stability and order-of-accuracy arguments presented in the text.
rationale.R1.3: The review needs to check if the reported benchmark speedups credibly support the claims of superior performance over fixed-splitting schemes.
rationale.R1.4: The review should evaluate the claimed novelty of the adaptive partitioning relative to standard fixed-splitting IMEX schemes.
rationale.R1.5: The effectiveness of the section's narrative arc, from method description to evaluation, is a key component of a review.
rationale.R1.6: A review typically includes feedback on sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and appropriate technical register.
rationale.RX.1: The agent must be transparent about any limitations in its ability to fully verify the mathematical claims from the text alone.
rationale.RX.2: The agent's review must not exaggerate flaws or strengths found in the artifact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must confine its review to the provided section and not speculate about the rest of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: The agent must ensure its feedback is constructive and avoids misrepresenting the work's quality in a way that could harm its reception.
rationale.RX.5: The agent's final review must clearly link its specific critiques and suggestions back to the provided text.
